title: M. Night Shyamalan's 'Glass' Is a Twist On Superhero Box Office Power | Exit Survey (Ep. 119)
format: Sean Fennessey and Justin Charity spoil M. Night Shyamalan's Glass, the psych-ward finale to the Unbreakable and Split trilogy, and doubt his claim that there won't be more.

films.title: Glass
films.note: The trilogy capper puts David Dunn, Mr. Glass, and the Horde in a psych ward with Sarah Paulson's Dr. Ellie Staple, who is there to convince them they aren't superheroes. Charity likes the muting of Samuel L. Jackson for the first half and the gaslighting premise, but both find the opening a disorienting bum-rush and Anya Taylor-Joy's Casey Cooke benched. Ends on four cliffhangers.

films.title: Unbreakable
films.note: Charity's favorite of the three: a superhero movie that sneaks its premise onto you, with the David Dunn / Elijah Price reveal landing in 2001, before the MCU.

films.title: Split
films.note: The stealth sequel both prefer to Glass, largely for McAvoy's Horde and Anya Taylor-Joy as Casey Cooke; the $100M hit that folded these characters back together.

films.title: The Sixth Sense
films.note: Fennessey's exception to Shyamalan's spotty record; Charity argues it's far more rewatchable than the twist-movie reputation suggests.

films.title: The Village
films.note: Part of the early successful run after Unbreakable.

films.title: Signs
films.note: Also from the early hit stretch.

films.title: The Happening
films.note: The start of the down period; cited as an all-time good-bad movie, their Bird Box comparison point.

films.title: Lady in the Water
films.note: Meant as a fairy tale for his children; read here as an angry vision of a self-styled genius under attack.

films.title: After Earth
films.note: The Will Smith misfire of the strange later stretch.

films.title: The Last Airbender
films.note: Widely expected to be good and wasn't; Charity skipped it, never having been into the animated Avatar.

films.title: The Visit
films.note: The small found-footage comeback that brought Jason Blum and Blumhouse into Shyamalan's orbit.
